Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»What time is Wayde van Niekerk in action on Wednesday night?

Wayde van Niekerk could be set for one of his final Olympic appearances on Wednesday night. Here is the exact time he’s in action.

Wayde van Niekerk hasmanaged to reach the200m  semi-finals, but his third position in a pedestrian time of20.42during the Olympic heats failed to inspire hope of the 32-year-old being able to compete for a medal.

Nevertheless, there will still be plenty of attention on how he performs in Wednesday night’s semi-final, in what could turn out to be his final Olympic appearance if he doesn’t progress to the final.

The 200m semi-finals start at 20:02, with Shaun Maswanganyi also vying for a place in the final, while Van Niekerk will follow soon after at 20:18.

ATHLETICS

Men’s 200m, semi-finals: (8.02pm):Shaun Maswanganyi

Men’s 200m, semi-finals: (8.18pm):Wayde van Niekerk

Women’s 400m, semi-finals: (9.01pm):Miranda Coetzee

DIVING

Women’s 3m springboard prelims (3pm):Julia Vincent

Team SA sprinter Benjamin Richardson has beenwithdrawn from Paris 2024and will take no further part in these Olympics.

In confirming the news, the Team SA chief medical officer Dr Jean de Wilde said: “Benjamin sustained a hamstring injury in the men’s 200m heats on Monday and has been withdrawn from all further competition.”

ATHLETICS

Women’s 400m heats, repechage:Miranda Coetzee ran a PB (personal best) 50.66 to finish second in her heat and qualify for the semi-final

Men’s 200m heats, repechage:Benjamin Richardson withdrawn

Men’s 400m semi-finals :Lythe Pillay finished seventh in his semi-final in 45.24 and didn’t progress to the final

Men’s 400m semi-finals:Zakhiti Nene finished sixth in his semi-final in 45.06 and didn’t progress to the final

Women’s 400m hurdles, semi-finals:Zeney Geldenhuys ran a PB (personal best) 53.90 in her semi-final in finishing third which placed her 10th overall and just out the final

Women’s 400m hurdles, semi-finals (8.17pm):Rogail Joseph ran a PB (personal best) 54.12 in her semi-final in finishing third which placed her 10th overall and just out the final

CANOEING

Men’s kayak, double 500m heats:Hamish Lovemore and Andrew Birkett finished fourth behind Australians Jean van der Westhuyzen and Tom Green, and progressed to the quarter-finals

Men’s kayak, double 500m quarter-finals:Hamish Lovemore and Andrew Birkett finished second behind the Spaniards Adrian Del Rio and Marcus Cooper and progressed to the semi-finals

Women’s kayak, double 500m heats:Tiffany Koch and Esti Olivier finished fifth in their heat in 1:52.14 and progressed to the quarter-finals

Women’s kayak, double 500m quarter-finals:Tiffany Koch and Esti Olivier finished sixth in their heat in 1:46.40 and were eliminated

SPORT CLIMBING

Women’s boulder & Lead, semi-final boulder:Lauren Mukheibir placed 20th with 0.00

Men’s speed, qualification:Joshua Bruyns finished 13th in the seeding qualification and went on to meet Amir Maimuratov of Kazakhstan in the heats. He was eliminated with a time of 5.84, with his opponent scoring 4.94
